name: Creamy Smoked Salmon and Dill Tart
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Fresh dill - chopped OR 1 Dill sprig
recipeInstructions:
1. Generously butter a 9-1/2-inch diameter deep-dish pie plate.
2. Place one phyllo sheet on a work surface (cover remaining pieces with plastic wrap, then with a clean damp towel).
3. Brush the phyllo sheet with butter and fold it in half lengthwise. Brush the folded surface with butter.
4. Cut the phyllo into halves crosswise.
5. Place one phyllo rectangle, buttered side down, in the prepared pie plate, covering the bottom and letting the pastry overhang by 1/2-inch on one edge.
6. Brush the top of the phyllo in the pie plate with butter.
7. Place a second phyllo rectangle in the pie plate, covering the bottom and letting the pastry overhang another section of the edge by 1/2-inch; brush with butter.
8. Repeat this process with the remaining 4 phyllo sheets, making sure the entire surface of the edge is covered to form the crust.
9. Fold any overhang under to form a tight crust edge along the pie plate edge.
10. Brush the crust edges with butter. (The tart can be prepared up to 4 hours ahead. Cover and refrigerate.)
11. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
12. In a medium bowl, whisk together the egg yolks, Dijon mustard, plus 1 teaspoon, eggs, half-and-half, whipping cream, smoked salmon, chopped green onions, and fresh dill (or 1 dill sprig).
13. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
14. Pour the mixture into the prepared crust.
15. Bake until the center is set, about 50 minutes.
16. Transfer to a rack and cool.
17. Garnish with dill sprigs and serve slightly warm or at room temperature.
